body: PT Karyaputra Suryagemilang, a subsidiary of PT Anabatic Technologies Tbk, has acquired 3.04% or 76 shares of PT Payrol Prima Indonesia for IDR 1.98 billion, aiming to expand its business portfolio and benefit from Payrol Prima's activities. As of March 31, 2024, Anabatic Technologies had IDR 653.29 billion in cash and cash equivalents, with total consolidated assets of IDR 4.17 trillion and total equity of IDR 449.34 billion.
title: PT Karyaputra Suryagemilang acquires 3.04% stake in PT Payrol Prima Indonesia to diversify business portfolio.

body: Nicke Widyawati, President Director of PT Pertamina, has been named one of Fortune's 100 most powerful women, ranking 47th, with the company expected to reap $75.8 billion in revenue in 2023, down 11% from last year. Under her leadership, Pertamina has focused on renewable energy and sustainable oil and gas technology investments, resulting in a profit of $4.77 billion in 2023, a 17% increase from 2022.
title: Pertamina's President Director Nicke Widyawati Ranked 47th in Fortune's 100 Most Powerful Women List.

body: The Indonesian government will hold an auction for government sukuk, targeting IDR 8 trillion, with 7 series of state sukuk offered, including 2 series of state treasury letters and 5 series of project-based sukuk, with returns ranging from discounts to 4.87% to 6.87%. The auction, to be held through the Bank Indonesia auction system, aims to fulfill part of the 2024 APBN financing target.
title: Indonesian Government to Hold Sukuk Auction Targeting IDR 8 Trillion Next Week

body: PT Megakreasi Cikarang Permai (MKCP) converted IDR 3.07 trillion (approximately USD 215 million) worth of long-term debt securities into 3.07 billion shares of PT Mahkota Sentosa Utama (MSU), equivalent to 45% of MSU's total equity as of June 2024. This transaction reduces MSU's debt burden and makes MSU a consolidated subsidiary of PT Lippo Cikarang Tbk (LPCK) via MKCP, indirectly benefiting LPCK.
title: PT Megakreasi Cikarang Permai converts long-term debt securities into 3.07 billion shares of PT Mahkota Sentosa Utama.

body: PT Mahakam Coal Terminal (MCT) will pay off its remaining obligations of $20.82 billion earlier than the due date, with its creditors being OCP Asia Fund IV and V. MCT will issue 34,200 new shares, with Alii taking 30,780 shares worth IDR 307.8 billion, as part of a voluntary prepayment plan funded by additional capital from its shareholders, totaling IDR 342 billion.
title: PT Mahakam Coal Terminal to Pay Off $20.82 Billion in Obligations Ahead of Schedule

body: The Jakarta Stock Price Composite Index (JCI) declined to 7,558.16 at the closing bell, despite a slight increase from the previous hour, and remained lower than its 9 am level of 7,597.42. Meanwhile, other indices such as LQ45, IDX30, IDX80, and IDXESGL reported gains, ranging from 1.26% to 1.80%, with some energy stocks correcting and others making strides.
title: Jakarta Composite Index Falls Despite Gains in Other Indices and Select Stocks

body: PT Bundamedik Tbk, the managing company of Bunda Hospital Network, plans to expand to untapped locations in Indonesia, aiming to add 1-2 hospitals each year, with a budget yet to be determined for next year's expansion. The company has already spent IDR 142 billion out of IDR 300 billion in capital expenditure this year, with the highest allocation going towards hospital renovations in Menteng, Palembang, and Bekasi.
title: PT Bundamedik to Expand Hospital Network to Untapped Locations in Indonesia Within 3-5 Years

body: PT Dosni Roha Indonesia TBK reported a 179% year-on-year increase in losses to IDR 166.11 billion in H1 2024, driven by a significant decline in revenue to IDR 338.29 billion, down from IDR 793.30 billion in H1 2023. The company's gross profit also plummeted 69% year-on-year to IDR 37.73 billion, with operating loss reaching IDR 114.10 billion, despite some optimization factors that helped mitigate the loss.
title: PT Dosni Roha Indonesia's loss surges 179% in H1 2024 due to significant revenue decline.

body: Foreign investors purchased a net 478.21 million shares, with notable buys including 951.53 million shares of PT Bumi Resources TBK, 745.07 million shares of PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia TBK, and 37.51 million shares of PT Estee Gold Feet TBK. Meanwhile, foreign investors sold a significant number of shares in companies such as PT Surya Citra Media TBK, PT Bank KB Bukopin TBK, and PT Bukalapak.com TBK, among others.
title: Foreign investors net buy 478 million shares in Indonesian market, led by purchases in coal mining and digital companies.

body: Miki Ojisan Indomitra released 440 million shares of PT Kurniamitra Duta Sentosa, reducing its ownership from 76.19% to 21.19%, while Dima Investindo acquired 55% of the shares to become the new controller. Meanwhile, several other companies, including PT Bayan Resources, PT Adaro Energy Indonesia, and PT Golden Eagle Energy, saw changes in their share ownership structures, with some experiencing reductions and others increasing their stakes.
title: Multiple companies in Indonesia undergo changes in ownership and shareholdings in various industries.

body: Indonesia recorded a deflation rate of 0.12% in September 2024, marking a five-month streak since May 2024, with prices decreasing mainly due to red chili pepper, birds' eye chili, fuel, and chicken products. Meanwhile, the annual inflation rate stood at 1.84% in September 2024, with a cumulative inflation rate of 0.74% since the beginning of the year, driven by increases in food, beverages, and housing-related expenditure groups.
title: Indonesia Records Five-Month Deflation Streak in September 2024 with 0.12% Decrease in Prices.

body: PT Trisula Internasional Tbk, a textile garment manufacturer, will distribute a cash dividend of IDR 7 billion, or IDR 2.25 per share, to its shareholders, following approval from the board of directors and commissioners. The company reported a net profit of IDR 21.27 billion as of June 30, 2024, with retained earnings of IDR 121.97 billion and total equity of IDR 741.86 billion.
title: Trisula Internasional to Distribute IDR 7 Billion Cash Dividend to Shareholders

body: PT Bank Syariah Indonesia (BSI) has collaborated with PT Prudential Sharia Life Assurance to expand its bancassurance services, expected to generate extra revenue from higher fee-based income, which reached IDR 2.48 trillion in the first half of 2024, up 28.01% year-on-year. This partnership aims to provide broader banking and financial services to BSI's 20.46 million users, solidifying its position as a financial partner and promoting national sharia financial inclusivity and literacy.
title: Bank Syariah Indonesia partners with Prudential Sharia Life Assurance to expand bancassurance services.

body: On Monday, September 30, foreign investors sold a net 587.45 million shares in the Indonesian stock market, with significant sales in banking companies such as PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia, PT Bank Central Asia, and PT Bank Mandiri. Meanwhile, foreign investors bought shares in companies like PT Bumi Resources Minerals, PT Buana Lintas Lautan, and PT Wulandari Bangun Laksana, resulting in net purchases of 210.86 million, 110.90 million, and 94.15 million shares, respectively.
title: Foreign investors sell majority of banking and coal mining shares, but buy into mineral mining and real estate sectors in Monday's trading.

body: International tourist visits in August 2024 reached 1.34 million, up 18.30% from August 2023, with Malaysia, Australia, and China being the top contributors, while total tourist visits until August 2024 rose 20.38% year-on-year to 9.09 million. Domestic tourist visits in August 2024 decreased 1.77% from July 2024, but surged 29.31% compared to August 2023, with a total of 674.60 million visits recorded from January to August 2024, up 19.20% year-on-year.
title: International tourist visits to Indonesia rise in August 2024, but still below pre-pandemic levels.

body: PT Garuda Indonesia's loss worsened by 32.88% year-on-year to USD 101.65 million in H1 2024, despite an 18.24% revenue growth to USD 1.62 billion, driven by increased revenue from scheduled flights, unscheduled flights, and other services. However, operating expenses surged 23.34% year-on-year to USD 1.53 billion, led by flight operation expenses, repair maintenance, and other costs, offsetting the revenue growth and contributing to the net loss.
title: Garuda Indonesia's loss widens to $101.65 million in H1 2024 due to increased operating expenses despite revenue growth.
